title: Oil Jumps as Violence Flares After Trump’s Offer to Help Ships Through Strait
summary: Oil prices fell and futures on the S&P 500 rose slightly on Sunday as investors reacted to continued uncertainty about the war in Iran.

title: Confusion in Strait of Hormuz Leaves Shipping Firms Guessing
summary: The U.S. vowed to help tankers navigate the perilous conditions that have kept them stranded in the Persian Gulf, but it remained unclear if companies would try to get out.

title: Supreme Court Temporarily Restores Access to Abortion Pill by Mail
summary: A lower-court ruling had reinstated a Food and Drug Administration requirement that patients visit a health care provider in person to obtain mifepristone.

title: Mifepristone, the Abortion Pill, Returns to the Supreme Court. Here’s What to Know.
summary: The Louisiana lawsuit, which seeks to block telehealth prescribing and mailing of the abortion pill mifepristone, has major implications for abortion access nationwide.

title: The U.S. Army’s ‘Big Experiment’ in the Arctic Cold
summary: How would soldiers from places like Florida, Texas and Georgia fight and persevere in temperatures as low as minus 40 degrees?

title: Judge Questions Why Correspondents’ Dinner Shooting Suspect Was Placed on Suicide Watch
summary: A federal magistrate ordered that the D.C. Department of Corrections explain why it had placed Cole Tomas Allen in especially restrictive conditions.

title: Rudy Giuliani Hospitalized in Florida in ‘Critical Condition’
summary: A spokesman for the former mayor of New York City said Mr. Giuliani was now breathing on his own.

title: The Met Gala’s Embrace of Jeff Bezos Causes a Backlash
summary: Protesters have found a perfect foil in Amazon’s founder, the gala’s lead sponsor this year.

title: The Met Makes a Statement With 9 New Mannequin Bodies
summary: The latest Costume Institute exhibition expands its ideas of who, exactly, belongs in fashion. Will the gala follow suit?

title: Met Gala 2026 Guide: Theme, Hosts, Dress Code and More
summary: What to know about this year’s celebration, including the return of a superstar last seen on the Met carpet 10 years ago.

title: Mark Carney Pulls Canada Closer to Europe as Both Struggle With Trump
summary: At a summit of European leaders, the Canadian prime minister was a special guest offering deals and friendship to jittery allies.

title: Europeans Prepare for a More Dangerous World in a Time of Economic Upheaval
summary: A stalwart on defense spending, Poland is training its citizens in civil defense while balancing their jobs and families.

title: Germans Are Not Panicking Over Trump’s Troop Threats
summary: Top leaders believe losing 5,000 U.S. troops will not hurt German security. But analysts fear an economic hit, especially in military communities.

title: More Than 150 Wind Projects Stall as Pentagon Delays Reviews
summary: The delays, which companies say have worsened significantly in recent weeks, are the latest step in the Trump administration’s efforts to block wind power.

title: Do Spinning Wind Turbines Really Mess With Radar Systems?
summary: As Pentagon officials delay approval of wind farms, scientific studies show that they can distort radar systems, but the problem is manageable, researchers say.

title: Home on the Range No More: Trump Wants Bison Gone
summary: The Trump administration is evicting bison herds from federal grasslands in Montana, siding with ranchers and Republican leaders over environmentalists and tribal leaders.

title: Trump Administration Orders Rapid End to Hunting Regulations on Federal Lands
summary: Internal Interior Department documents show parks are imposing sweeping changes like expanding areas where firearms can by used and allowing game to be cleaned in restrooms.

title: Chasing Stardom in Korea Nearly Destroyed Ejae. Then Came ‘KPop Demon Hunters.’
summary: Ejae, the movie’s breakout star, was the face of K-pop in 2025. Facing questions of art, national belonging and authenticity, she wants to be much more.

title: The Return for These Investors Isn’t Money, It’s More Affordable Housing
summary: Local governments are trying to create housing that is permanently affordable by investing directly in construction. They are rewriting how housing programs have traditionally operated.

title: A 22,000-Home Community Is Being Built in a 5,000-Person Town
summary: Chatham Park, a master-planned community in Pittsboro, N.C., is expected to expand the town to 60,000 people when completed.

title: A Medical Examiner Chases Down an Elusive Killer
summary: Unusual opioids are infiltrating street drugs. Knoxville’s top forensic doctor is on the front lines, pressing to quickly identify the most lethal.

title: What to Know About Orphines, a New Class of Deadly Opioids
summary: The drugs are 10 times more dangerous than fentanyl. They are showing up in street drugs in the South and the Midwest, and will most likely spread to other regions.

title: Is Loving Food the Secret to Eating Less of It?
summary: The case for ditching self-denial and embracing enjoyment.

title: Modi’s B.J.P. Wins West Bengal State Election for the First Time
summary: The party of Prime Minister Narendra Modi took control of West Bengal for the first time and made gains around the country. Its defeated opponents accused it of cheating.

title: What to Know About the Suspected Hantavirus Outbreak on an Atlantic Cruise Ship
summary: Three passengers of the MV Hondius died after showing symptoms of the rare disease. Health authorities confirmed one case and are investigating five others.

title: What Is Hantavirus, Which Is Linked to the Deaths of 3 People Aboard a Cruise Ship?
summary: Three people aboard a cruise ship sailing the Atlantic Ocean have died from what health officials suspect were hantavirus infections.

title: John Sterling, the Yankees’ Most Enduring Announcer, Dies at 87
summary: He called over 5,600 games, more than any other announcer for the team, and was known for his personalized, slightly wacky home run calls.

title: Streisand, LuPone, Holliday, Menzel: Broadway’s Biggest Voices
summary: Lea Michele, Christine Ebersole, Jennifer Hudson, Kristin Chenoweth, Adam Lambert and 10 others reflect on their favorite belters.

title: Speak, Yuppie
summary: The young urban professionals of the 1980s changed our cities, our politics and our meritocratic work lives.

title: Operation Epic Fury, Meet Operation Colossal Blunder
summary: With the threat of a future closing of the Strait of Hormuz a drone strike away, the Iranians appear to actually hold the cards.

title: Asked and Answered
summary: We dig into reader questions.

title: With World Distracted by Iran War, Israeli Settlers Intensify Attacks in West Bank
summary: Israel has failed to arrest a wave of violence against Palestinians, prompting military officials to urge the government to intervene.

title: School Cellphone Ban Study Finds Mixed Results
summary: Cellphone bans got devices out of students’ hands, according to the first large study. But behavior and academics have not improved, at least so far.

title: Doris Fisher, Co-Founder of the Gap, Dies at 94
summary: The company she started in 1969 with her husband, Don, grew from a single store selling jeans and records to a $16 billion brand that remade the apparel industry.
